overall_cve.delta_vs_prior_run: April MTD moved 3885 (day 21 snapshot) → 4227 (day 22 snapshot): +342 CVEs net over 24h. Most of that growth is NVD back-population of 2026-04-21: that day's count is now 359 (vs 35 carried forward by yesterday's same-day query). Apr 22 same-day reading is 18 entries. Full-month extrapolation up from 5547 → 5764 (+3.9%). Daily rate steady at ~192/day. W17 partial (days 1-3) = 543.

kev_additions.reconciliation_note: No new KEV entries between 2026-04-20 and 2026-04-22 — catalog refreshed to version 2026.04.21 but content unchanged from prior run. Total remains 22 for April, ahead of Q1 2026 monthly average (~24) with 8 days remaining.
notable_entries: CVE-2026-20122 / 20128 / 20133 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager cluster (2026-04-20). Cisco is a Glasswing participant and these are HTTP-parsing-adjacent management-plane bugs. KEV listing implies active exploitation before disclosure, which disqualifies them from the probable-participant-self-scan bucket per current criteria.

CVE-2026-34197 — Apache ActiveMQ (2026-04-16) — per Horizon3.ai disclosure, found by Naveen Sunkavally using Claude (~10 min attack-chain trace). Claude-assisted but NOT directly credited to Anthropic/Carlini.

confirmed_glasswing: CVE-2026-4747 (FreeBSD NFS RCE, ~17-year-old bug — Anthropic/Carlini autonomous find)
adjacent_claude_credited: CVE-2026-5194 (wolfSSL cert validation — Mythos Preview-assisted, Carlini @ Anthropic)
claude_assisted_non_anthropic: CVE-2026-34197 (Apache ActiveMQ — Horizon3.ai / Naveen Sunkavally using Claude as tool; NOT direct Anthropic credit)
glasswing_cves.source: The Register, InfoQ, CSO Online, Conzit, Tech-Insider coverage 2026-04-15..22. No new direct-attribution CVEs detected in this run. InfoQ cites 'CVE-2026-31402' as a Linux NFS heap overflow finding by Carlini with Claude Code (23-year-old bug) — worth investigating whether this is a separately-assigned CVE or conflated with CVE-2026-4747 FreeBSD. Flagging for analyst review.
glasswing_cves.notes: Holding official count at 40 pending additional direct-attribution CVE IDs. 'Five kernel vulnerabilities' claim is still the boundary of public attribution; only CVE-2026-4747 is formally enumerated. Today's Cisco SD-WAN Manager KEV cluster is NOT being counted toward Glasswing (active-exploitation evidence fails the 'no prior attacker exploitation' criterion for probable-participant-self-scan).

ms_patch_tuesday.ranking: Second-largest Patch Tuesday ever per Redmondmag; shy of October 2025 (167).

rhel8_errata.source: Estimate retained from prior run. stack.watch parent page continues to return only YTD aggregate. Red Hat security data API still 404s.
rhel8_errata.confidence: low — placeholder; true April count likely 20-30 but not precisely determinable from available sources

weekly_cve.observation: W17 ran 543 across Apr 20-22, ~181/day vs W16's final 168/day. The acceleration is mostly back-population: Apr 21 moved from 35 → 359 overnight as NVD caught up; Apr 22 same-day is only 18. Extrapolating the 3-day 543 across 7 days gives ~1,267 — would be the lowest full week of the month. Too early to call an inflection; expect W17 final count to come in around 900-1000 after normal back-population.

exploit_intelligence.summary: 10 of 14 watch list CVEs have confirmed public exploit availability. 5 at weaponized (CVE-2026-39987, -32201, -34197, -34621, -33825), 1 functional (CVE-2026-21858), 4 at PoC/circulating (CVE-2026-40477, -40478, plus weaponized) and 4 no public exploit (wolfSSL pair + Cisco ISE/Webex). No maturity-level escalations since prior run (no new items added, no movement on existing items). Counter-argument: absence of public PoC for wolfSSL / Cisco ISE does not imply absence of private exploitation — state actors and advanced red teams typically hold exploits.

vendor_advisory_flags.notes: Unauthenticated path traversal in LogScale (HTTP-adjacent). CrowdStrike is a Glasswing participant. NVD-published 2026-04-21. Critical candidate for probable-participant-self-scan if no third-party credit appears on CrowdStrike's advisory. Analyst should verify credit line and exploitation evidence.

Symlink escape in Claude Code sandbox (sandbox → workspace breakout). Anthropic is the Glasswing principal, not just a participant. Local attack vector reduces HTTP-adjacency score. NVD-published 2026-04-21.

vendor_advisory_flags.cwe: Auth bypass via crafted HTTP request
vendor_advisory_flags.notes: Cisco (Glasswing participant); HTTP-adjacent auth bypass via password change handling. Disclosed early April 2026. Analyst should check for researcher credit.
Cisco (Glasswing participant); unauthenticated RCE, HTTP-adjacent management plane. Analyst should verify credit and disclosure cluster.
Chromium upstream CVE pass-through — not a self-scan candidate. Listed only to confirm vendor advisory channel was checked.
NVIDIA (Glasswing participant). Deserialization flaw is a known automated-scan bug pattern. Not primarily HTTP-parsing but network-adjacent. Analyst should verify credit.

AWS (Glasswing participant); arbitrary host file overwrite via symlink in Firecracker jailer. Published earlier in 2026 but surfaced in this advisory sweep. Local attack vector reduces HTTP-adjacency score.

np_di_candidates.rationale: Unauthenticated path traversal on HTTP-adjacent product from a Glasswing participant vendor. CrowdStrike has a large enterprise footprint and this is exactly the pattern the NP+DI thesis predicts. Also a top probable-participant-self-scan candidate — analyst should confirm credit line.
Critical OS command injection in CI/CD product with broad enterprise deployment. CWE-78 is a canonical NP+DI pattern.
Anthropic (Glasswing principal) self-discovery of a sandbox symlink escape. Local attack vector — doesn't fit the HTTP-parsing watch list scope, but worth noting as a participant-self-scan signal.
Unauthenticated SQLi in headless commerce platform; HTTP API surface. Lower deployment footprint than enterprise products, monitor for exploitation signals.
Error-based SQL injection in the shape sync API. Emerging product; monitor adoption trajectory.
CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated code injection via WebSocket JSON relay. Pattern matches Marimo CVE-2026-39987 (also WebSocket-adjacent).
Zip-slip path traversal in module install feature. Classic NP+DI pattern.
Path traversal in MCP server over HTTP transport. MCP ecosystem is expanding fast; watch for exploitation of exposed SSE endpoints.
Regional Asia-Pacific office automation product with limited English-language exposure. NP+DI fits but deployment footprint too narrow for watch list.
shell_exec() on GraphQL mutation fields — classic NP+DI. FreePBX has a meaningful telephony-admin footprint.

projection_comparison.commentary: April 2026 extrapolates to ~5,764 — ~500 below the conservative May anchor (6,264) and ~2,000 below the aggressive May anchor (7,764). Extrapolation rose from 5,547 → 5,764 overnight as NVD back-populated Apr 21 (35 → 359). Daily rate is steady at ~192/day. April is tracking ~9% below March 2026 (6,304) — the gap is narrowing as back-population fills in. No Glasswing volume inflection visible yet. Counter-argument: NVD publishing latency (4–8 weeks) means Glasswing-era CVEs from the April 7 launch largely haven't surfaced yet; first meaningful volume signal still expected in May–June 2026.

counter_arguments: CVE volume has NOT spiked post-Glasswing launch (April 7) through day 22: April extrapolates to ~5,764, ~9% BELOW March's 6,304 and well below all +500 to +5,000/mo projections. Two reads: (a) NVD publishing latency is 4–8 weeks so Glasswing discoveries may not appear yet; (b) Glasswing disclosures are being staged through coordinated vendor patches, so they'll arrive lumpily.

Daily publication rate has stabilized near ~190/day. Today's +342 over 24h is a back-population artifact — Apr 21 alone moved from 35 → 359 as NVD caught up. The underlying pace is still well below the aggressive +2-5k/mo thesis.
The March 2026 spike to 6,304 (+57.1% YoY) clearly predates Glasswing and is driven by CNA expansion, automated fuzzing, and broader disclosure norms — not AI discovery.

CISA KEV April additions are flat at 22 today (catalog 2026.04.21 refreshed but unchanged content-wise). Still pacing near the Q1 average (~24/mo). KEV additions are editorial decisions by CISA, not purely volume-driven. Five of 22 April entries are legacy re-adds (2009, 2012, 2020, 2023 CVEs) reflecting catalog housekeeping.
Microsoft's 163-CVE April Patch Tuesday is notable but a single data point; October 2025 already hit 167 pre-Glasswing, so high-volume Patch Tuesdays aren't novel.

Weekly CVE data: W14 186/day, W15 231/day, W16 168/day (final), W17 partial ~181/day over 3 days (back-population expected to lift this). W16 — the first FULL week post-Glasswing — came in as the slowest of the three measured weeks. If Glasswing were already inflating publication volume, we'd expect W16 to be higher, not lower.

NP+DI candidate sweep turned up CVE-2026-40050 (CrowdStrike LogScale, unauth path traversal, CVSS 9.8) — CrowdStrike is a Glasswing participant. If the analyst can confirm no third-party researcher credit on CrowdStrike's advisory, this is a strong probable-participant-self-scan candidate. Counter-argument: CrowdStrike has a mature internal security team and could easily find this without AI assistance; correlation with Glasswing timing is suggestive, not causal.

Exploit maturity on watch list remains bimodal: 5 weaponized (all already KEV-listed), 2 with functional/circulating PoCs (Thymeleaf SSTI, n8n), and 4 with no public exploit (wolfSSL pair + 4 Cisco ISE/Webex). Counter-argument: absence of public PoC doesn't mean absence of exploitation — private exploits and state-actor tooling are invisible to this survey.

Other side of the argument: CVE-2026-39987 (Marimo) was exploited within 10 hours of disclosure. Short window from disclosure-to-weaponization is a real phenomenon independent of Glasswing and affects watch list risk calculus regardless of AI attribution.
